title: Create and host a2acli CIMD document (GitHub Pages)
description:
The mithlond consent SPA fetches client_id as a URL at runtime (CIMD pattern). a2acli's client_id must be a real HTTPS URL pointing to a metadata.json with client_name and redirect_uris. This is a BLOCKER for the auth-code+PKCE flow.

CIMD DOCUMENT CONTENT:
{
  "client_id": "https://ghchinoy.github.io/a2acli/metadata.json",
  "client_name": "a2acli",
  "client_uri": "https://github.com/ghchinoy/a2acli",
  "logo_uri": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ghchinoy/a2acli/main/docs/assets/logo.png",
  "redirect_uris": ["http://127.0.0.1:8080/callback"],
  "grant_types": ["authorization_code"],
  "token_endpoint_auth_method": "none",
  "scope": "agent:invoke"
}

HOSTING: docs/metadata.json served via GitHub Pages.

GITHUB PAGES SETUP:
The a2acli repo already has a docs/ directory used for documentation markdown. GitHub Pages configured to serve from /docs on main branch will serve docs/metadata.json at https://ghchinoy.github.io/a2acli/metadata.json — the exact URL to use as client_id.

Steps:
1. Add docs/metadata.json with content above.
2. Add docs/.nojekyll (empty file) so GitHub Pages serves JSON files without Jekyll processing.
3. Enable GitHub Pages in repo Settings > Pages > Source: Deploy from branch > Branch: main > Folder: /docs
4. Verify https://ghchinoy.github.io/a2acli/metadata.json is reachable and CORS-accessible (GitHub Pages sends Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * by default).
5. Record the URL in a2ac-38z implementation as the hardcoded client_id.

NOTE: If a custom domain (e.g. a2acli.dev or docs.a2acli.dev) is preferred over ghchinoy.github.io/a2acli, add a CNAME file to docs/ and configure DNS. For now, the GitHub Pages URL is sufficient.

title: OAuth 2.1 token acquisition (auth-code + PKCE + CIMD) from AgentCard security schemes
description:
Real-world A2A servers (e.g. eldamo-server on Cloud Run) gate the protocol with OAuth 2.1 and advertise it via the AgentCard OAuth2SecurityScheme. a2acli is currently passthrough-only (--token/--auth); users must mint JWTs out-of-band. Add a client-side OAuth flow so a2acli can obtain a token itself.

== CONCRETE TARGET ==
Eldamo Cloud Run: https://eldamo-mcp-server-308690897031.us-central1.run.app
Auth server base: https://www.mithlond.com/mcp-auth
Token endpoint:   https://www.mithlond.com/mcp-auth/api/oauth/token

eldamo-server Phase 4 (l04.4) will land the OAuth2SecurityScheme in the AgentCard, at which point a2acli can read it automatically. Until l04.4 deploys, the validated handshake is:
  export ELDAMO=https://eldamo-mcp-server-308690897031.us-central1.run.app
  set -a; source .env; set +a && TOKEN=$(make token)   # in eldamo-server repo
  a2acli discover --service-url $ELDAMO
  a2acli send 'Namarie' --service-url $ELDAMO --transport jsonrpc --wait --token "$TOKEN"
  a2acli send 'name star silver quenya' --service-url $ELDAMO --transport jsonrpc --wait --token "$TOKEN"

== DESIGN DECISIONS ==
1. CIMD (Client Instance Metadata Document) where available.
   OAuth 2.1 auth servers may expose /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server (RFC 8414)
   and optionally a registration_endpoint (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration).
   Flow:
     a. Read AgentCard OAuth2SecurityScheme -> get Oauth2MetadataURL
     b. GET Oauth2MetadataURL -> AS metadata: authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint,
        registration_endpoint (if present), code_challenge_methods_supported
     c. If registration_endpoint present -> dynamically register (CIMD path):
        POST registration_endpoint with client_name, redirect_uris, etc.
        -> receive client_id (and optionally client_secret)
     d. If no registration_endpoint -> fall back to requiring --client-id flag
   'CIMD where available' means: attempt dynamic registration first; fall back gracefully.

2. Auth-code + PKCE as the primary interactive flow.
   - Generate code_verifier (32 random bytes, base64url) + code_challenge (S256)
   - Spin up a local HTTP callback server on a random available port
   - Build authorization_url: authorization_endpoint + code_challenge + redirect_uri + state
   - Open browser (platform-specific: 'open' macOS, 'xdg-open' Linux, 'start' Windows)
   - Wait for the callback, extract code + verify state
   - POST to token_endpoint: code + code_verifier + client_id + redirect_uri -> access_token (+ refresh_token)
   - Kill local server

3. Client Credentials flow for non-interactive (service-to-service):
   a2acli auth login <url> --client-id <id> --client-secret <secret>
   -> POST token_endpoint -> access_token; no browser, no callback.

4. Token storage alongside existing XDG config.
   Store in ~/.config/a2acli/tokens/ keyed by service URL host.
   Format: JSON with access_token, refresh_token (if any), expiry, client_id.
   Security: file permissions 0600 (cross-platform baseline; no platform keychain for now -- revisit if users request it).
   Auto-refresh: if refresh_token present, attempt refresh before expiry; fall back to re-auth.
   Auto-use: 'a2acli send' checks the token store for the target URL before requiring --token.

5. Commands:
   a2acli auth login <service-url>                              # interactive auth-code+PKCE
   a2acli auth login <service-url> --client-id <id> [--client-secret <s>]  # client credentials
   a2acli auth status <service-url>                             # show token validity + expiry
   a2acli auth logout <service-url>                             # delete stored token
   a2acli auth token <service-url>                              # print current access_token (for scripts)

== DEPENDENCIES ==
- eldamo-server l04.4 for the AgentCard OAuth2SecurityScheme (AgentCard read path; rest works against the token endpoint directly)
- New dep: no heavy OAuth library needed. The flow is ~200 lines of net/http + crypto/sha256 + encoding/base64.
- No browser-automation dependency; just os/exec to open the URL and a net/http listener for the callback.

== ACCEPTANCE ==
- 'a2acli auth login $ELDAMO' opens a browser, completes the PKCE flow, stores the token.
- Subsequent 'a2acli send ... --service-url $ELDAMO' uses the stored token automatically (no --token flag).
- 'a2acli auth status' shows expiry and whether the token is valid.
- 'a2acli auth logout' clears the stored token.
- 'a2acli auth token' prints the raw JWT (for make token / shell scripting equivalence).
- CIMD path: when eldamo's registration_endpoint is present, a2acli registers itself and does not require --client-id.
- Non-CIMD path: without registration_endpoint, a2acli requires --client-id (with clear Hint: if omitted).
- Client credentials flow works for non-interactive environments (CI, agents).

== RELATED ==
- a2ac-9xv (exploratory persistent auth) -- this task supersedes it; close 9xv when 38z ships.
- eldamo-server l04.4 (AgentCard OAuth2SecurityScheme deployment)
- a2ac-26a (discover security scheme display -- already improved; 38z builds on it)
design: Read SecuritySchemes from the resolved AgentCard; for OAuth2 authorizationCode, run a loopback redirect + PKCE flow (RFC 8252), discovering endpoints from the card and/or the authorization-server metadata. Cache/store the resulting token (tie into a2ac-9xv persistent auth). Fall back to existing --token passthrough.
acceptance_criteria: a2acli can authenticate to an OAuth2-protected A2A server end-to-end without a pre-supplied token; token reused across invocations; documented with the Eldamo server as the worked example.

title: Streaming send fails in non-interactive shells: 'could not open a new TTY'
description: Found while testing against the Eldamo A2A server (github.com/ghchinoy/eldamo-server). Default 'a2acli send' uses streaming mode which launches the Bubble Tea TUI; in a non-interactive shell / CI / agent context it aborts with: 'Alas, there's been an error: could not open a new TTY: open /dev/tty: device not configured'. Today the only workaround is --wait or --immediate. Streaming should degrade gracefully when there is no TTY.
design: Detect TTY (e.g. isatty on stdin/stdout). When absent, auto-fall-back to a plain line-streamed renderer (print events as they arrive) instead of starting Bubble Tea. Optionally add an explicit --plain/--no-tui flag and honor NO_COLOR/CI env. Keep TUI as default only when interactive.
acceptance_criteria: a2acli send <msg> (no flags) against a streaming A2A server works in a piped/non-TTY shell, printing streamed events to stdout with exit 0; TUI still used when interactive; --plain forces non-TUI.
close_reason: Fixed: TTY detection in resolveOutputMode() auto-degrades from tui to text when stdout is not a terminal. Also detects CI env var. Explicit --output flag always honoured regardless of TTY state.

title: Add --verbose / -v global flag
description:
Add a --verbose / -v global persistent flag that emits diagnostic information to stderr without disturbing stdout output. This is the missing flag from both proposals (#1929 and discussion #306) and is immediately useful when debugging against real agents (a2a-simple, syntaxis).

Stderr output when --verbose is set (examples):
  [verbose] resolving agent card from http://localhost:9001
  [verbose] auto-selected transport: grpc
  [verbose] sending message id=msg-abc task=task-xyz skill=project_create
  [verbose] received TaskStatusUpdateEvent state=TASK_STATE_WORKING
  [verbose] received TaskArtifactUpdateEvent artifact=result.pdf bytes=42301

Key design rules:
- Verbose output ALWAYS goes to stderr; structured output (--output json) stays on stdout.
- --verbose composes cleanly with --output json: scripts get clean stdout, humans see debug on stderr.
- NO_COLOR does not suppress verbose; A2ACLI_VERBOSE=true should activate it as env var.
- Respect the 'Hint:' convention for any verbose error context.

References: syntaxis already uses --verbose to stream agent logs to stderr ('syntaxis execute my_paper --verbose'). Match that ergonomic expectation.
close_reason: Implemented --verbose / -v persistent flag and A2ACLI_VERBOSE=true env var. Verbose output goes to stderr. Also improved discover security scheme display using type switch (HTTPAuth/OAuth2/APIKey/OpenIDConnect/MutualTLS) — prompted by eldamo-server's OAuth 2.1 AgentCard.

title: Target resolver core (keystone)
description:
Build the unified target resolver that every agent-facing command (discover, send, subscribe, get, cancel, download) routes through. This is the keystone of ARD integration — everything else hangs off it.

A 'target' is resolved by form:
- Raw URL (http://, https://) -> direct (current behavior)
- Bare host (acme.com) -> try /.well-known/agent-card.json, then /.well-known/ai-catalog.json
- Local file (./card.json, ./catalog.json) -> read from disk
- ARD URN (urn:air:publisher:ns:name) -> resolve via configured catalog/registry
- name@catalog (assistant@acme) -> resolve from a named catalog
- Bare name (translator) -> search configured registries (may disambiguate)

Requirements:
- Replace/augment the current --service-url with a target concept; keep --service-url working.
- Protocol-neutral internally: entries carry an IANA media type; invocation dispatched by type. A2A entries are invocable; non-A2A (MCP/skill) are resolvable/displayable but clearly 'not invocable here'.
- Respect --output tui/text/json and existing precedence (flags > env > config > defaults).
- Clear errors + Hints when resolution fails or is ambiguous.

title: Add push-notification config commands (push-config create/list/get/delete)
description:
a2acli does not currently expose the A2A TaskPushNotificationConfig surface, which is part of the spec we otherwise test thoroughly. This is the most significant functional/conformance gap.

Add commands mapping to the A2A push notification RPCs:
- push-config create <task-id> <callback-url> [--auth-scheme <scheme>] [--auth-credentials <creds>] — SetTaskPushNotificationConfig
- push-config list <task-id> — ListTaskPushNotificationConfigs
- push-config get <task-id> <config-id> — GetTaskPushNotificationConfig
- push-config delete <task-id> <config-id> — DeleteTaskPushNotificationConfig

Reference: a2a-rs/a2acli implements create/list/delete. Should respect --output tui/text/json and global auth flags. Add e2e conformance coverage against the TCK SUT if it supports push config.

Rationale: completeness is a2acli's differentiator as a spec-testing instrument; this fills a real protocol gap.

title: Add discover/subscribe as primary command names, keep describe/watch as aliases
description: Both issue #1929 and discussion #306 use 'discover' (not 'describe') and 'subscribe' (not 'watch'). Cobra supports aliases via the Aliases field on cobra.Command. Switch to discover/subscribe as primary names and add describe/watch as backwards-compatible aliases. Update help text, README, skills, and conformance tests accordingly.
close_reason: discover/subscribe now primary; describe/watch kept as aliases via Cobra Aliases field

title: Improve Artifact Retrieval UX: Add 'download/retrieve' command, enable saving from 'status', and add --file flag
Add --wait / --sync flag to 'send' command for blocking execution returning only final results
Restructure CLI commands to align semantics (send, watch, get, chat, cancel, config)
Documentation housekeeping: sync docs with v1.8.0 features
description: README, COMPARISON.md, TEST_AGENTS.md, SKILL.md + references, and CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md had stale content predating discover --extended (a2ac-o2i), multi-modal send (a2ac-79d), list tasks filters (a2ac-mvu), config env (a2ac-4x2), and the eldamo extended-card shipment (eldamo-server-lde). Fix stale entries, remove duplicate o2i in COMPARISON Planned Alignments, regenerate CONFORMANCE_REPORT at v1.8.0.
close_reason: Updated README (discover --extended, list --context/--status), COMPARISON.md (multi-modal done, removed completed+duplicate o2i from Planned Alignments, added extended-card/multi-modal/config-env to leads), TEST_AGENTS.md (eldamo extended card live, a2a-simple gRPC in progress, refreshed gaps/feature-map), SKILL.md + describe.md/list.md references, regenerated CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md at v1.8.0 (all PASSING).

title: Add CI conformance workflow (pinned test-server checkouts + e2e)
description:
a2acli has NO CI workflow running conformance today — .github/workflows/ has only release.yaml and verify-winget.yml. The e2e tests are local integration tests that t.Skipf when sibling repos (../apex, ../github/a2a-go) are absent, which they always are in GitHub Actions. So 'CI conformance' is currently aspirational/manual (make test-e2e).

PROPOSAL: add .github/workflows/conformance.yml that:
1. Checks out the test-server repos at PINNED commits/tags:
   - a2aproject/a2a-go (TCK SUT) — pinned
   - ghchinoy/a2a-experiments (a2a-simple: grpc-echo, multimodal, extended-card) — pinned
   - apex a2a_a2ui (A2UI extension) — pinned (if public)
2. Runs make test-e2e with the appropriate *_SRC env vars pointing at the checkouts.
3. Optionally runs make conformance-report and uploads it as an artifact.

WHY PINNING MATTERS: reproducible conformance requires locking both server behavior
AND fixture bytes. This pairs with the deterministic-asset design in the a2a-simple
multimodal server (a2a-simple-lin) — pinned server + committed assets = deterministic,
assertable CI. A pinned server with runtime-generated assets would still be non-deterministic.

DEPENDENCY: most valuable once a2a-simple ships the fixture servers (grpc-echo,
multimodal, extended-card). Can start with just the TCK SUT + apex A2UI that already exist.

Consider: add A2A_SIMPLE_SRC env var to the Makefile alongside A2A_GO_SRC so the
multimodal/grpc-echo e2e tests can locate the a2a-simple checkout (mirror the
existing apex-path-discovery pattern in conformance_test.go).

title: Add e2e coverage for all artifact types + task states (consume a2a-simple multimodal)
description:
SERVICE RELOCATED: the a2a-multimodal kitchen-sink server is now built in a2a-simple (a2a-simple-lin). This task is the a2acli-side e2e coverage:

- Add e2e tests against the a2a-simple multimodal server validating:
  * binary artifact save (a2ac-mfd) across Text/Data/Raw/FileURL
  * task-state handling: working, input-required, auth-required, completed, failed, canceled
- Gated on a2a-simple-lin shipping.

See docs/TEST_AGENTS.md.

title: Add e2e coverage for gRPC/REST transports (consume a2a-simple grpc-echo)
description:
SERVICE RELOCATED: the a2a-grpc-echo server is now built in a2a-simple (a2a-simple-4e1), per the decision that deterministic test fixtures live in the A2A Experiments repo, not a2acli. This task is now the a2acli-side e2e coverage:

- Add e2e tests that spin up the a2a-simple grpc-echo server and validate:
  * transport auto-selection (gRPC > JSONRPC > REST)
  * --transport grpc / jsonrpc / rest explicit selection
  * a2acli's gRPC and REST client paths against a real (non-TCK) agent
- Follow the existing e2e pattern (start server, waitForServer, runCLI assertions)
- Gated on a2a-simple-4e1 shipping.

See docs/TEST_AGENTS.md.

title: Persist Raw and FileURL artifact parts in client saveArtifact (audio/binary support)
description: a2acli's client-side saveArtifact (cmd/a2acli/main.go ~1060) currently writes only Text parts (and Data as JSON); it ignores a2a.Part Raw bytes and URL parts. As a result, when consuming agents that return binary artifacts (e.g. read-aloud's A2A service returning an MP3 as NewRawPart(MediaType audio/mpeg) or NewFileURLPart(gcsURL,audio/mpeg)), the audio is not saved to disk. PROPOSAL: extend saveArtifact to (1) write Raw bytes to <artifact.Name or generated>.<ext-from-MediaType> at 0644; (2) for URL parts, download via the existing http client (respecting auth) and save, or at minimum print the URL; (3) infer file extension from MediaType (audio/mpeg->.mp3, audio/wav->.wav, etc.) with a fallback to artifact Filename. Add an e2e check against a binary-returning agent. Context: read-aloud is adding an A2A serve mode (read-aloud-ijo epic) intended to be consumed by a2acli.

title: Extend serve --echo to reflect all received part types (Option A for a2ac-79d)
description:
Extend 'a2acli serve --echo' to reflect DataParts and FileParts back to the caller in addition to TextParts. Currently the echo server only echoes text.

This provides a self-contained, zero-dependency test target for a2ac-79d (multi-modal --parts/--json/--file/--data input): send a multi-modal message, verify the echoed response contains the same part types and content, assert round-trip fidelity without needing an external server.

Implementation in cmd/a2acli/serve.go echoAgentExecutor.Execute():
- Iterate all message parts
- For TextPart: echo as TextPart (current behaviour)
- For DataPart: echo back as DataPart with same value and mediaType
- For FilePart/RawPart: echo back with same bytes and mediaType

Test:
  a2acli serve --echo &
  a2acli send --parts '[{"data":{"foo":"bar"},"mediaType":"application/json"}]' --wait --output json
  # assert response artifact contains the same DataPart

Companion to a2a-simple Option B task (a2ac-79d multimodal_echo skill).

title: Documentation update: OAuth 2.1 auth-code+PKCE+CIMD flow
description:
Update all documentation and skill files to reflect the shipped OAuth 2.1 auth-code + PKCE + CIMD flow (a2ac-38z). Verify changes against the live candir.mithlond.com test target before merging.

== MUST UPDATE ==

README.md (Commands section):
- Add 'auth' command group with login/status/logout/token subcommands + examples
- Update Quick Start to show the auth-first workflow:
    a2acli auth login --service-url https://candir.mithlond.com
    a2acli send 'name star silver quenya' --env mithlond --wait
- Add 'mithlond' named environment to the Configuration Management example (config.yaml)
- Update the non-interactive mode note: agents should use
  '--token $(a2acli auth token -u ...)' since auth login requires a browser
- Add transport field to the config file example (new feature)

README.md (Global Flags table):
- Add auth token store note: stored tokens auto-used when --token absent

skills/a2acli/SKILL.md:
- Update global flags table: remove '--token is required for auth' language
- Add auth section: auth login (interactive, one-time), auth token (for agent scripting)
- Note that auto-token means agents need not pass --token after auth login

skills/a2acli/references/auth.md (NEW FILE):
- Full auth workflow for AI coding agents
- When to use auth login vs --token directly
- Token storage location: ~/.config/a2acli/tokens/ (keyed by host)
- Scripting: TOKEN=$(a2acli auth token -u <url>) for non-interactive contexts
- CIMD: what it is, why client_id is a URL, where a2acli's is hosted

docs/COMPARISON.md ('Where a2acli Leads' section):
- Add OAuth 2.1 + PKCE + CIMD entry. Only comparable CLI: spec-works/a2a-ask
  (also has OAuth2), but a2acli adds CIMD dynamic client identity (no pre-registration
  required; consent SPA fetches client metadata at runtime from CIMD URL).

docs/RELEASING.md:
- Note that docs/metadata.json (CIMD) is served via GitHub Pages from /docs.
  The client_id URL is stable across releases. Do not change it without updating
  all OAuth registrations.

== CLOSE WHEN DONE ==
- Close a2ac-9xv (exploratory persistent auth) as superseded by a2ac-38z.

== VERIFICATION STEPS (run before marking done) ==
1. a2acli auth login --service-url https://candir.mithlond.com
2. a2acli auth status --service-url https://candir.mithlond.com   # shows valid token
3. a2acli conformance --service-url https://candir.mithlond.com   # Auth Gating: PASS (not SKIP)
4. a2acli send 'name star silver quenya' --env mithlond --wait    # uses stored token via env
5. a2acli auth token -u https://candir.mithlond.com               # prints raw JWT
6. TOKEN=$(a2acli auth token -u https://candir.mithlond.com)
   a2acli send 'Namarie' --service-url https://candir.mithlond.com --token "$TOKEN" --wait

title: Document and handle fixed callback port constraint (127.0.0.1:8080)
description:
The mithlond consent SPA's metadata.json pre-registers specific redirect URIs:
  http://localhost:8080/callback
  http://127.0.0.1:8080/callback
  http://127.0.0.1:8080/mcp/oauth/callback

a2acli's local callback server MUST bind to 127.0.0.1:8080 at /callback. Port randomisation (the usual robust approach) is not possible without a new redirect URI being registered in mithlond-web's metadata.json.

IMPLICATIONS FOR IMPLEMENTATION:
- Attempt to bind 127.0.0.1:8080 before starting the auth flow.
- If port 8080 is already in use, fail with a clear error and Hint: 'free port 8080 and retry, or contact the server administrator to register an additional redirect URI'.
- Do NOT silently try alternative ports — the auth server will reject them.
- Prefer 127.0.0.1 over localhost to match the registered URIs exactly (some OAuth servers check strictly).

FUTURE: If mithlond-web registers additional ports/paths in metadata.json, the implementation can be updated to probe available ports in the registered list.

title: Add A2UI v1.0 extension conformance validation (and report it)
description:
Phase 0 (workstream 2) of the cross-project A2UI v0.9 -> v1.0 effort. Make a2acli a transport/wire-level CONFORMANCE HARNESS for the A2UI A2A extension, so A2UI sample servers (e.g. apex/tools/sample_servers/a2a_a2ui) can be proven conformant WITHOUT a Flutter renderer. A2UI is transport-decoupled and rides inside A2A DataParts, so a2acli (already A2A v1.0 PASSING, built on a2a-go) is the natural place to verify it.

== ARCHITECTURE DECISION (cross-cutting): shared conformance engine ==
a2acli is growing THREE objective, schema-driven conformance surfaces that all ride on A2A and all ship formal JSON Schemas:
  1. base A2A conformance  -> 'conformance' command (shipped, a2ac-afq) + TCK e2e
  2. A2UI extension wire    -> THIS task
  3. ARD catalog/registry   -> a2ac-alu.7
These MUST share one internal validation engine, not three bespoke validators. Build it here, generically, so a2ac-alu.7 reuses it:
  - New package: internal/conformance (schema-driven validator + the conformanceResult shape already used by cmd/a2acli/conformance.go).
  - Embed JSON Schemas via //go:embed; validate with github.com/santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v6 (draft 2020-12).
  - Emit the existing conformanceResult{Name,Passed,Message,Skipped} struct so all conformance surfaces compose and report identically.
Keep the a2ui command concrete now, but the ENGINE generic (schema set + activation rules are inputs). Future: unify under a 'conformance' umbrella (conformance smoke|a2ui|catalog|registry) once a third lands -- do NOT pre-build that abstraction, just keep output shapes consistent.

Distinguish from two NON-goals of this task:
  - Inline validation (DX hygiene) during normal 'catalog fetch'/'search'/'discover' is a separate, lightweight use of the same engine -- tracked with ARD, not here.
  - Audit (advisory DX/AX quality: descriptions present, examples populated, agent-legibility) is a separate advisory surface, not conformance. Out of scope here.

== FEASIBILITY (verified against a2a-go v2 v2.2.0) ==
- AgentCapabilities.Extensions []AgentExtension exists (URI, Params, Required, Description) -> read the A2UI extension decl + surface params.supportedCatalogIds / params.acceptsInlineCatalogs.
- Part.MediaType carries the MIME; Data.Value holds the payload; Part.Metadata / Message.Metadata / Message.Extensions carry extension metadata (incl. a2uiClientDataModel echo).
- Extension activation header (X-A2A-Extensions) rides on the existing --svc-param/paramInterceptor plumbing; add convenience to set it automatically in a2ui mode.

== RISK TO VERIFY EARLY ==
The spec text says assert 'metadata.mimeType == application/a2ui+json', but a2a-go exposes Part.MediaType (not part metadata). Before writing the assertion, capture a real payload from apex/tools/sample_servers/a2a_a2ui and confirm whether the MIME is on Part.MediaType or Part.Metadata['mimeType']. This determines the read path. Cheap to check, expensive to get wrong.

== SCHEMA HANDLING: VENDOR, do not fetch at runtime ==
For a certification tool, reproducibility is the point. Vendor the A2UI v1.0 JSON Schemas into the repo (e.g. internal/conformance/schemas/a2ui/v1_0/) and embed with //go:embed. Record the upstream a2ui-project/a2ui commit/version in a header. Offline-capable, version-pinned, deterministic.

== SCOPE ==
Phase A (ship first; unit-testable against vendored schemas + CAPTURED FIXTURE payloads, no live server needed -- decouples from apex-2xl):
  1. 'a2acli a2ui validate --service-url ...' (dedicated 'a2ui' command group, parallels push-config):
     a. Read AgentCard; check AgentCapabilities.extensions for uri=https://a2ui.org/a2a-extension/a2ui/v1.0; surface params.supportedCatalogIds + params.acceptsInlineCatalogs.
     b. Send X-A2A-Extensions activation header (JSON-RPC/HTTP) or gRPC metadata.
     c. Capture returned DataParts; assert MIME == application/a2ui+json (location per risk check above); data is an ARRAY of messages; each envelope version == v1.0.
     d. Validate each message against vendored A2UI v1.0 schemas (server_to_client_list.json; client_to_server_list.json; catalog_definition.json; client_capabilities.json; client_data_model.json).
     e. Per-message PASS/FAIL; CONTINUE on failure (list is non-transactional; atomicity is per-message).
     f. Respect --output tui/text/json and --verbose; non-zero exit on any FAIL.
Phase B (needs a server that implements them):
  2. Round-trips: action(wantResponse,actionId)->actionResponse; callFunction->functionResponse/error(INVALID_FUNCTION_CALL); sendDataModel echo in message metadata (a2uiClientDataModel).
Phase C (certification story):
  3. Add 'A2UI Extension v1.0' section to docs/CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md + the make conformance-report flow.
  4. e2e test analogous to TestConformance, running against apex a2a_a2ui sample server as SUT (test dependency, like the TCK SUT -- NOT a feature dependency). Gate live e2e on apex-2xl; until then use captured fixtures for unit tests.
  5. Document usage in README.md and add to docs/COMPARISON.md as the flagship 'beyond base TCK' differentiator (certifies base A2A AND the A2UI extension, renderer-free).

== DEPENDENCIES ==
- New dep: github.com/santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v6
- Vendored A2UI v1.0 schemas from a2ui-project/a2ui specification/v1_0/json/
- Live e2e gated on apex-2xl (sample server v1.0 migration); Phase A unit tests use captured fixtures and are NOT blocked.
- Engine designed for reuse by a2ac-alu.7 (ARD conformance).

== ACCEPTANCE ==
- a2acli validates a running A2UI v1.0 server end to end, clear PASS/FAIL per check, non-zero exit on failure.
- A deliberately non-conformant payload (old application/json+a2ui MIME; single object instead of array; version v0.9) is correctly flagged.
- CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md gains an auto-generated A2UI Extension v1.0 result block.
- Validation engine lives in internal/conformance and is consumable by ARD conformance (a2ac-alu.7).

== REFERENCES ==
- A2UI v1.0 spec: https://github.com/a2ui-project/a2ui/tree/main/specification/v1_0
- A2A extension spec: specification/v1_0/extensions/a2a/docs/a2ui_extension_specification.md
- v1.0 JSON schemas: specification/v1_0/json/
- SUT + manual test plan: ../apex/tools/sample_servers/a2a_a2ui/ (V1_CONFORMANCE_TESTPLAN.md), apex bd issue apex-2xl
- a2a-go SDK: github.com/a2aproject/a2a-go
close_reason: Phase A complete: a2ui validate command + shared internal/conformance engine. Schema-driven validation against vendored A2UI v1.0 schemas (//go:embed, santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v6). Validates: AgentCard extension declaration, metadata.mimeType==application/a2ui+json, data is v1.0 envelope array, server_to_client_list schema. 7 unit tests (conformant + legacy-MIME, wrong-location, non-array, wrong-version, no-parts negatives). MIME-location risk RESOLVED: v1.0 is metadata.mimeType not Part.MediaType. Live-verified extension detection against apex a2a_a2ui server. Phase B (round-trips) and Phase C (CONFORMANCE_REPORT section + live e2e gated on apex-2xl) remain as follow-ups.

title: Improve discover security scheme display (OAuth2 scopes, token URL, API key location)
description:
Discovered via eldamo-server Phase 4 (l04.4) roadmap: when an AgentCard declares full OAuth 2.1 security scheme metadata (token endpoint, scopes, metadata URL), a2acli discover should surface this clearly in text mode.

Current: shows HTTP scheme type and bearerFormat (fixed in a2ac-2os), OAuth2 type and Oauth2MetadataURL. Missing:
- OAuth2 flow details (clientCredentials.tokenUrl, authorizationCode.authorizationUrl, scopes)
- Per-skill scope requirements (which scopes are needed for THIS skill specifically)
- APIKey: header/query name shown but not Hint about how to pass it via a2acli flags

Target output (text mode) for a skill with OAuth2 + scopes:
  - [translate] Translate (Sindarin → Quenya)
    Security: oauth2 [scopes: eldamo:translate]
    Hint: Get a token from https://eldamo.example.com/api/oauth/token and pass via --token

Verbose mode should additionally show full flow details (tokenUrl, authorizationUrl).
The --output json mode already exposes everything via the raw AgentCard JSON.

Test against: eldamo-server once Phase 4 (l04.4) ships.

title: Add 'conformance' smoke command for validating real A2A servers
description: Pragmatic real-world validation: a single command that runs discover -> assert AgentCard well-formed -> assert auth gating (401 without token, 200 with token) -> send round-trip. Useful for CI against servers like Eldamo and as an A2A spec conformance smoke check.
design: a2acli conformance --service-url <url> [--token <jwt>] running an ordered checklist with PASS/FAIL per step and a non-zero exit on failure; --json output for CI. Reuse existing resolver/client code paths.
acceptance_criteria: Running conformance against a local Eldamo instance reports discover/auth/send results; exits non-zero on any failure; --json machine-readable.
close_reason: Implemented: 'conformance' command runs three smoke checks (AgentCard well-formed, auth gating, round-trip send). Respects --output json for CI. Exit code non-zero on any failure. Registered under Client Configuration group.

title: Actionable 401 guidance: parse WWW-Authenticate and point to auth endpoints
description: When a protocol call returns 401 (e.g. Eldamo /a2a without a token), a2acli currently surfaces a generic 'unexpected HTTP status: 401 Unauthorized'. It should parse the WWW-Authenticate header and the AgentCard/oauth-authorization-server metadata to tell the user exactly how to authenticate (which scheme, which authorize/token endpoint, and the --token hint).
acceptance_criteria: On 401, a2acli prints the realm/scheme from WWW-Authenticate and a concrete next step (run OAuth flow, or pass --token); covered by a test against a 401-returning stub.

title: Registry search client (search/explore/list)
description:
Dynamic discovery against an ARD Registry REST API. NOTE: this is the v0.9 DRAFT surface — isolate the wire format behind an interface so a spec revision is a localized change.

Commands:
- a2acli search "<query>" [--registry NAME] [--filter type=...,tags=...] [--federation auto|referrals|none] [--page-size N]
    -> POST /search ; render ranked results (score, identifier, type, url, source)
- a2acli explore [--registry NAME] [--facet type] [--facet publisher]   -> POST /explore (facets)
- a2acli registry list <url> [--filter ...] [--order-by ...]            -> GET /agents

Handle the query model (text + filter), federation modes, pagination (pageToken), and referrals. Filter keys are dot-paths into catalog entries. Respect --output.

title: Static catalog support (fetch/list/validate)
description:
Handle ARD static catalogs (the stable, registered layer — no Draft-API risk).

Commands:
- a2acli catalog fetch <url|file>   : GET /.well-known/ai-catalog.json (or read file), display host + entries
- a2acli catalog list <url|file>    : list entries (table for tui/text, array for json)
- a2acli catalog validate <url|file>: validate against ARD JSON Schema + semantic rules (URN format, value-or-reference exclusivity, representativeQueries sizing)

Parse the ai-catalog.json data model: specVersion, host{displayName,identifier}, entries[]{identifier(URN), displayName, type(media type), url XOR data, description, tags, capabilities, representativeQueries, version, trustManifest}. Handle nested catalogs (type application/ai-catalog+json) and inline data vs url.

title: Catalog/registry config + URN resolution
description:
Make catalogs and registries first-class config alongside named environments.

Extend ~/.config/a2acli/config.yaml:
  catalogs: { acme: 'https://acme.com/.well-known/ai-catalog.json' }
  registries: { public: 'https://...', corp: 'https://...' }
  default_registry: corp
  # an env may carry a registry/catalog instead of a service_url
  envs: { corp: { registry: 'https://registry.internal.corp/api/v1/' } }

Commands:
- a2acli catalog add <alias> <url>     : register a catalog alias
- a2acli catalog remove <alias>

Resolution: URN (urn:air:acme.com:agent:assistant) and name@catalog (assistant@acme) resolve through configured catalogs to an agent card URL, then hand off to the resolver core. Wire into resolver core target forms.

title: ARD (Agentic Resource Discovery) integration
description:
Incorporate the Agentic Resource Discovery spec (https://github.com/ards-project/ard-spec) so a2acli becomes a single tool for BOTH discovery and interaction of A2A agents (and, longer term, other agentic resources).

Design north star: discovery and use merge via a smart TARGET RESOLVER, not a parallel command silo. Every command that takes an agent target accepts richer forms (URL, host, file, ARD URN, name@catalog, bare name) and the resolver figures out the rest. Catalogs/registries become first-class config alongside named environments. The existing Bubble Tea TUI becomes a discovery browser (discover -> select -> view card -> converse).

Phasing is risk-aware: ARD is v0.9 Draft (IANA media types pending, wire format may change), so build the stable static-catalog + resolver foundation first and isolate the Draft registry REST API behind an interface.

Design doc: docs/ARD_INTEGRATION.md
Naming note: as scope expands beyond A2A (MCP servers, skills in catalogs), the resolver/catalog core is kept protocol-neutral so an eventual rename (e.g. 'agents') is packaging, not rearchitecture.

title: Add discover --extended for authenticated extended agent card
description:
Add a --extended flag to 'discover' that fetches the authenticated extended AgentCard via the A2A GetAuthenticatedExtendedCard RPC, rather than the public well-known card.

  a2acli discover --extended --token <TOKEN>
  a2acli discover --extended --auth 'Bearer ...'

Behavior:
- Requires auth (token/auth headers); error with a clear Hint if none provided.
- Falls back to / errors clearly if the agent does not advertise support for an extended card.
- Respects --output tui/text/json.

Reference: a2a-rs/a2acli supports requesting an extended card. Small, spec-relevant, useful for testing agents that expose richer cards to authenticated callers.

title: Support reading message from stdin on send
description:
Allow piping a message into 'send' via stdin, matching ericabouaf/a2a-cli ergonomics:

  echo 'test message' | a2acli send
  cat prompt.txt | a2acli send

Behavior:
- If no positional message arg is given AND stdin is not a TTY, read the full message text from stdin.
- If both a positional arg and piped stdin are present, positional wins (or document precedence clearly).
- Works with --output json/text/tui and --wait/--immediate.

Rationale: natural fit for the scripting/agent story and test pipelines. Low effort, high ergonomic win. Currently only --instruction-file reads from a file; raw stdin is the missing piece.
close_reason: Implemented: read from stdin when no positional arg given and stdout is not a TTY. Args validator updated to require arg only when interactive. verboseLog reports bytes read.

title: Add --output flag with tui/text/json modes
description:
Add a --output <mode> global flag supporting three output modes: 'tui' (default, interactive Bubble Tea), 'text' (non-interactive human-readable, no animations, for CI/piped output), 'json' (machine-readable NDJSON, equivalent to current -n/--no-tui).

Design decisions:
- Long form only (--output), no -o shorthand — -o is already taken by --out-dir on send/get/watch/download
- Keep -n/--no-tui as a backward-compatible alias for --output json (do not remove)
- NO_COLOR=true and A2ACLI_NO_TUI=true should map to --output text rather than --output json (plain human-readable, not machine-readable)
- 'text' mode is the non-interactive human experience: formatted output, no spinners/animations — equivalent to what git does when stdout is not a terminal

Aligns with the --output/-o flag proposed in a2aproject/A2A#1929 and a2aproject/a2a-go discussion #306. The -o shorthand conflict is a known gap vs those proposals.

title: Add multi-modal message input to send (--parts, --json, --file, --data)
description:
Add structured and multi-modal message input to 'send', so a2acli can test agents that accept more than plain text.

PRIMARY TEST TARGET: syntaxis's 'project_add_source' skill accepts file content as a message part. This is the concrete use case that drives this feature — without it, you can only send syntaxis plain text prompts, not actual source material.

Flags:
- --parts <json>  : raw JSON parts array; CLI wraps in ROLE_USER Message
- --json <body>   : raw JSON A2A Message object (full control)
- --file <path>   : attach a file as a FilePart (repeatable)
- --data <json>   : attach a structured DataPart (repeatable)

Behavior:
- Plain text positional arg remains the default/simple path.
- --file and --data compose with a text message (multiple parts in one Message).
- Respects --output tui/text/json.

References: discussion #306 (--parts/--json), spec-works/a2a-ask (--file/--data).

title: Add send --immediate flag (fire-and-forget)
description: Both issue #1929 (--return-immediately) and discussion #306 (--immediate) specify a fire-and-forget mode for send: set ReturnImmediately in SendMessageConfig and return the Task ID immediately without waiting for completion or streaming. The user can then use 'get' or 'watch' to follow up. Currently a2acli only supports blocking (--wait) or streaming (default).
close_reason: Added --immediate flag to send; sets ReturnImmediately:true and returns task ID immediately

title: Add --timeout global flag
description: Discussion #306 specifies a global --timeout <dur> flag with a default of 30s. Currently a2acli has no request timeout, which means hung agents block indefinitely. Add as a persistent flag on rootCmd, apply to all HTTP/gRPC client calls. Should accept Go duration strings (e.g. 30s, 2m).
close_reason: Implemented in v1.4.4: --timeout duration flag applied to getResolver() HTTP client

title: Update a2a-go SDK to latest version and fix tests
Refactor colorizedHelpFunc to use semantic tokens instead of hardcoded ANSI/lipgloss colors
Implement adaptive/theme-aware coloring for optimal contrast in both Light and Dark terminal modes
Audit a2acli help and TUI output to maximize data-ink ratio (remove non-essential color/borders)
Introduce semantic color tokens (Accent, Muted, Pass, Warn, Fail) for consistent styling
Audit and synchronize flag short-names across all commands
Standardize 'Hint' guidance for common error states (missing config, invalid IDs)
Implement basic semantic help styling (colorized headers and flags)
Add 'Long' descriptions and 'Example' blocks to all core commands
Organize a2acli commands into logical groups (Tasks, Info, System)
Update README.md documenting how --wait/--sync maps to A2A SendMessage Blocking:true parameter
Update README.md with new CLI command semantics (send, watch, get)
Implement 'cancel' command to terminate tasks
Configuration Management (XDG + Named Server Environments, --config)
Phase 3: Migrate a2acli to use the a2a-go SDK release/spec-v1 branch
Phase 2: Add TCK SUT to Makefile and implement baseline conformance tests using os/exec
Phase 1: Add non-interactive mode (--no-tui) and JSON output for CLI testability
Add golangci-lint configured to align with Google Go standards
In addition to .env config, let's use XDG base directory configs
Implement discover --extended e2e (consume a2a-simple extended-card)
description:
SERVICE RELOCATED: the a2a-extended-card server is now built in a2a-simple (a2a-simple-2z1). This task pairs with a2ac-o2i (the discover --extended feature). Once a2a-simple-2z1 ships:

- Implement a2ac-o2i (discover --extended flag) if not already done
- Add e2e test against the a2a-simple extended-card server
- Gated on a2a-simple-2z1.

See docs/TEST_AGENTS.md. Relates to a2ac-o2i.

title: Add --full / --no-truncate flag to show complete artifact content in TUI/text mode
description:
Currently displayTaskResult truncates artifact text at 500 characters and shows a Hint about --out-dir. This is intentional for large payloads (prevents terminal flooding), but users want a flag to see the full content inline without saving to a file.

Add a --full flag (or --no-truncate) to send/get/download:
  a2acli send '...' --wait --full     # shows complete artifact text, no truncation
  a2acli get <taskID> --full

Behaviour:
- Without --full: current 500-char preview with Hint (unchanged)
- With --full: print the complete artifact text to stdout, no truncation
- In --output json: truncation never applies (raw JSON is always complete, unchanged)
- In --output text: apply the same full vs truncated logic as TUI

Discovered during live testing of candir.mithlond.com translate skill:
  'translate hello friend to Sindarin' returned a truncated response in TUI mode.
  The full lexicon analysis and translation is valuable to read inline.

title: config env list with token store status
description: Implement 'a2acli config env list' showing each named environment, its service URL, transport (if set), and whether a valid (non-expired) token is stored in ~/.config/a2acli/tokens/. Tabular output in text mode, JSON array in --output json. Lets users see at a glance which envs are ready to use without re-authenticating.

title: config env add/remove/use commands
description: Implement 'a2acli config env add <name> --service-url <url> [--transport <t>]', 'config env remove <name>', and 'config env use <name>'. Read/write ~/.config/a2acli/config.yaml using viper. Validate that service-url is reachable (optional --no-verify flag to skip). After adding an env, print a one-liner showing how to use it.

title: Interactive config management (a2acli config env add/remove/list)
description:
Make named environments first-class via the CLI — no manual YAML editing. Currently users must directly edit ~/.config/a2acli/config.yaml to add, update, or remove named environments. This creates friction for new users and is error-prone.

Commands:
  a2acli config env add <name> --service-url <url> [--transport <t>]
      Adds or updates a named environment in config.yaml.
  a2acli config env remove <name>
      Removes an environment (with confirmation if it is the default_env).
  a2acli config env list
      Lists all configured environments with their service URL and whether a token is stored.
  a2acli config env use <name>
      Sets default_env in config.yaml.

Integration with auth:
  After 'a2acli auth login --service-url <url>', suggest:
    'Run: a2acli config env add <name> --service-url <url>  to save this as a named env.'
  After adding an env, 'a2acli auth login --env <name>' works naturally.

Discovery integration (future, after ARD):
  a2acli config env add --search 'translation agent'  -> discovers via ARD, adds resolved URL.

title: A2UI conformance Phase C: report integration + live e2e
description:
Follow-up to a2ac-8qf (Phase A shipped). Certification-story tasks:
1. Add an 'A2UI Extension v1.0' section to docs/CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md and the make conformance-report flow (auto-generated PASS/FAIL block).
2. Add an e2e test analogous to TestConformance that runs 'a2ui validate' against the apex a2a_a2ui sample server as SUT. GATED on apex-2xl (sample server must emit conformant v1.0: metadata.mimeType=application/a2ui+json, array payload, v1.0 envelopes) AND the apex server's gob-serialization bug ('gob: type not registered for []map[string]interface{}') must be fixed so DataParts actually return. Until then Phase A unit tests (captured fixtures) provide coverage.
Reuse internal/conformance/a2ui.
close_reason: Phase C complete: integrated A2UI v1.0 extension results into docs/CONFORMANCE_REPORT.md via 'make conformance-report'. Added live e2e integration test in e2e/conformance_test.go that spins up the apex a2a_a2ui server dynamically, runs 'a2ui validate --output json', and asserts that the card, MIME, shape, and version checks pass but schema validation correctly fails on the 'h1' variant schema issue (verifying the validator's precision).

title: A2UI conformance Phase B: client->server round-trip validation
description:
Follow-up to a2ac-8qf (Phase A shipped the validate command + engine). Add round-trip conformance checks for A2UI v1.0 client->server flows:
- action(wantResponse,actionId) -> actionResponse
- callFunction -> functionResponse / error(INVALID_FUNCTION_CALL)
- sendDataModel echo in message metadata (a2uiClientDataModel)
Validate client->server messages against vendored client_to_server_list.json. Needs the multi-modal --data/--parts input from a2ac-79d to send client->server DataParts, and a server that implements these flows. Reuse internal/conformance + internal/conformance/a2ui.

title: ARD conformance testing (catalog + registry)
description:
a2acli's signature move applied to ARD: be the Go-native conformance tester for ARD manifests and registries (mirrors how it already tests A2A v0.3/v1.0).

Commands:
- a2acli conformance catalog <url|file> : validate ai-catalog.json vs ARD JSON Schema + semantic rules
- a2acli conformance registry <url>     : probe POST /search, POST /explore, GET /agents for spec compliance (status codes, pagination envelope, score range, entry structure)

SHARED ENGINE: reuse internal/conformance built in a2ac-8qf (A2UI extension conformance) -- the schema-driven validator (//go:embed schemas + santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v6) and the conformanceResult{Name,Passed,Message,Skipped} output shape. Do NOT build a bespoke ARD validator; supply ARD's JSON Schemas (CDDL/JSON Schema/OpenAPI) as inputs to the shared engine. This keeps base-A2A, A2UI, and ARD conformance composable and identically reported.

INLINE vs DEDICATED: discovery commands (catalog fetch / search) should call a lightweight well-formedness check from the same engine and surface a Hint on malformed responses (DX hygiene); this dedicated 'conformance' command runs the exhaustive validation for certification/CI. Audit (advisory DX/AX quality) is a separate future surface, not part of this task.

Reference ARD's own conformance tool (Python) and schemas for parity. Add e2e coverage. Depends on static catalog + registry client (a2ac-alu.2, a2ac-alu.4) and the shared engine from a2ac-8qf.

title: TUI discovery browser (discover -> select -> converse)
description:
Turn the existing Bubble Tea TUI into an interactive discovery browser — the daily-driver experience that no other community CLI has.

Flow: 'a2acli search "translation agent"' (no --output) renders ranked results in the TUI; arrow-select an entry; view its AgentCard (skills, security, transports); then act inline ('Send a message?') which streams the response in the same session.

This is the discovery HALF that composes with the conversation/REPL half (a2ac-srx). Together they form discover -> select -> view -> converse without leaving the tool. Depends on resolver core + registry search.

Coordinate scope with a2ac-srx (chat REPL): the browser handles discover+select; a2ac-srx handles the multi-turn conversation loop after selection.

title: Local cache / known-agents store
description:
Bridge discovery -> repeated use by caching resolved cards locally (XDG cache dir), keyed by URN. Mirrors agntcy .a2aagents/ and spec-works install model.

Commands:
- a2acli catalog pull <alias>   : fetch + cache all entries from a catalog for fast/offline use
- a2acli agents                 : list locally-known (cached) agents
- resolver checks cache first for URN/name targets -> instant, offline-capable

Include cache invalidation (updatedAt/ETag where available) and a way to clear the cache.

title: Add list tasks filters: --context, --status, --since, --with-artifacts
description: Discussion #306 specifies richer filtering for 'list tasks': --context <id> (filter by context ID), --status <state> (filter by task state, short forms: submitted/working/completed/failed/canceled/rejected), --since <time> (RFC 3339, only tasks updated after this timestamp), --with-artifacts (include artifacts in response), --history <n> (include history messages per task). Currently a2acli only supports --limit and --page-token.

title: Implement serve --exec mode
description: Discussion #306 specifies an exec mode for 'serve': wrap any subprocess as an A2A agent. Message text goes to stdin, stdout becomes the response artifact. Exit code 0 = TASK_STATE_COMPLETED, non-zero = TASK_STATE_FAILED. Supports a --chunk=<delimiter> flag for streaming output incrementally as ArtifactUpdateEvents. The flag exists in code but is marked 'not yet supported'.

title: Implement serve --proxy mode
description: Discussion #306 specifies a proxy mode for 'serve': forward all requests to an upstream A2A agent, log traffic to stderr, inject service parameters and auth headers. The flag exists in code but is marked 'not yet supported'. Implementation should create an a2aclient.Client for the upstream, forward each A2A operation, inject --svc-param values via AttachServiceParams, and derive the proxy's own AgentCard from the upstream card with the local address substituted.

title: Expose A2A CLI as an MCP server via STDIO (--mcp)
Support dynamic transport selection (gRPC vs JSONRPC) based on AgentCard SupportedInterfaces
Interactive Chat Mode REPL using Bubbletea
serve mock ARD catalog + registry
description:
Extend 'serve' so a2acli can stand up the full ARD test topology with no external deps:
- a2acli serve --catalog <file>  : host a static /.well-known/ai-catalog.json
- a2acli serve --registry <file> : mock registry exposing POST /search, POST /explore, GET /agents over an index

Combined with 'serve --echo' (the agent under test), a single binary plays all three roles: agent + catalog + registry. Enables a self-contained discover->resolve->invoke e2e conformance fixture. Depends on static catalog + registry client knowledge.

title: Explore persistent auth / token storage integrated with environments
description:
EXPLORATORY (not committed). spec-works/a2a-ask has the richest auth of any community CLI: OAuth2 device-code + client-credentials flows, encrypted token storage (DPAPI on Windows, 0600 files elsewhere), auto-refresh, per-issuer client registration, and per-tenant token separation.

a2acli already has named environment profiles (--env) and a config file. Persistent auth could COMPLEMENT environments: e.g. store a token/credential per named environment so 'a2acli send --env prod' transparently authenticates.

Open questions to resolve before committing:
- Is persistent SSO actually needed for a TESTING tool, or is per-invocation --token/--auth sufficient?
- Scope: just token storage keyed to environments, or full OAuth2 device-code/client-credentials flows?
- Security: how to store credentials safely cross-platform without a heavy dependency.

Decision: keep as p4 backlog/idea until a concrete need appears. The simpler, higher-value adjacent win is making 'discover' surface security schemes clearly (helps users know WHAT auth an agent needs) — partially done today, could be polished. See also a2ac-o2i (discover --extended).

Reference: spec-works/a2a-ask auth login/logout/status, register-client/list-clients/remove-client.
